---
title: "Visualization"
---

# Visualization {#sec-visualization}

The `plot.sc_fit()` method ships two variants in v0.1. The default
`"beta_ridgelines"` shows one density ridgeline per attribute dummy
over the held-out per-respondent
$\hat\beta_j(Z_i)$; it is the most compact visual summary of the
direction-versus-intensity decomposition described in
@sec-interpreting. `"loss_trace"` plots the per-fold training loss
curves and is useful when diagnosing non-convergence.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(sconjoint)
```
:::


Both variants return a plain `ggplot` object, so users can layer
additional geoms, facets, or themes on top. The ridgeline layer is
drawn by `ggridges::geom_density_ridges` when `ggridges` is
available; a boxplot fallback kicks in otherwise so the plot method
still works in minimal installations.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
cat("visualization\n")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
visualization
```


:::
:::
